profile.name: Rober
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; employed; tends to manage the day-to-day tracking of accounts and investment decisions.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High (works in finance; comfortable with account types, tax implications, and portfolio discussions, and will often sanity-check numbers out loud).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Growth-oriented and willing to tolerate market swings if the plan is coherent; prefers taking risk in diversified, explainable ways and gets uneasy with vague or opaque strategies.
profile.communication_style: Direct and detail-focused; will quote specific balances and correct small inaccuracies. Uses quick clarifiers like “to be precise” and occasionally pauses to do mental math; asks pointed questions about taxes and account ownership.
emotional_tendencies: relief when there is a clear checklist
profile.decision_making_style: Research-and-verify; wants to see options, assumptions, and tradeoffs before agreeing, then moves quickly once convinced.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open; trust increases with transparency, clear fee/tax explanations, and follow-through on action items.
internal_biases: recency bias after strong or weak market periods
hidden_concerns: wants reassurance that the household is on track for retirement without needing to take on debt
concerned about tax drag in the taxable brokerage and whether they are missing easy optimizations
quietly worried about health habits affecting long-term planning

profile.name: Mary
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; big-picture planner for lifestyle and future goals; participates actively but often lets Robert lead on technical details.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate (understands budgeting and broad investing concepts, but mixes up terms like gross vs. net or account types; prefers plain-English explanations and round numbers).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Generally comfortable with growth if there’s a safety buffer; reacts more to the idea of “running out” than to short-term volatility, and will push for clarity on cash reserves and monthly spending coverage.
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; uses hedging like “I think” and “roughly,” and may ask the advisor to restate in simpler terms. Occasionally interrupts to confirm what a term means, then defers back to Robert for specifics.
emotional_tendencies: stress when conversations get too technical
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants to hear the recommendation, then checks how it affects day-to-day life and flexibility before agreeing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-positive; builds trust through patience, clear summaries, and not talking down when she asks basic questions.
internal_biases: loss aversion framed as fear of lifestyle disruption
hidden_concerns: wants clarity on how their income is structured and what is truly reliable month to month
concerned about not having formal protection/insurance in place but unsure what they actually need
worries about making a mistake in the taxable account that creates an unexpected tax bill
